ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Exchange w3wp.exe → LOLBin/credential-dump child (ProxyLogon webshell post-exploitation)
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical HAFNIUM/Operation Exchange Marauder kill chain: ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) drops an ASPX webshell (China Chopper-style) into an Exchange virtual directory; the IIS Exchange app pool worker (w3wp.exe) then spawns cmd/PowerShell/procdump/7z/certutil to enumerate, dump LSASS, archive, and exfiltrate. This parent→child shape is the highest-fidelity Silk Typhoon signature and persists across their later Exchange/Ivanti/Citrix zero-day campaigns (Microsoft tracking ID G0125 / Hafnium / Silk Typhoon).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=w3wp.exe (Processes.parent_process=*MSExchangeOWAAppPool* OR Processes.parent_process=*MSExchangeECPAppPool* OR Processes.parent_process=*MSExchangeServicesAppPool* OR Processes.parent_process=*MSExchangeOABAppPool* OR Processes.parent_process=*MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool* OR Processes.parent_process=*MSExchangeRpcProxyAppPool*) (Processes.process_name IN (cmd.exe,powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,net.exe,net1.exe,whoami.exe,ipconfig.exe,systeminfo.exe,nltest.exe,quser.exe,tasklist.exe,procdump.exe,procdump64.exe,rundll32.exe,7z.exe,7za.exe,certutil.exe,bitsadmin.exe,curl.exe,wget.exe,makecab.exe)) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest_host | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — IIS Exchange app pool spawning recon / credential-theft / archive LOLBins
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
// Anchor on the Exchange app pools — ProxyLogon webshells live in OWA/ECP/Autodiscover/Services
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (
    "MSExchangeOWAAppPool","MSExchangeECPAppPool","MSExchangeServicesAppPool",
    "MSExchangeOABAppPool","MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool","MSExchangeRpcProxyAppPool")
| where FileName in~ (
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe",
    "ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe",
    "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe",
    "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","makecab.exe")
| extend SuspiciousCmd = case(
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass","comsvcs.dll","MiniDump"), "LSASS dump",
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","-enc ","DownloadString","Invoke-Expression","IEX("), "PS download cradle",
    FileName in~ ("7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any (".aspx",".log",".pst",".eml","\\Mailbox\\"), "Exchange data archiving",
    FileName in~ ("certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"), "LOLBin egress",
    "recon")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildPath = FolderPath, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          SuspiciousCmd, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — Exchange w3wp.exe post-exploitation child processes
// Requires GPO 'Include command line in process creation events' so 4688 carries CommandLine.
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| where ParentProcessName has_any (
    "MSExchangeOWAAppPool","MSExchangeECPAppPool","MSExchangeServicesAppPool",
    "MSExchangeOABAppPool","MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool","MSExchangeRpcProxyAppPool")
  or CommandLine has_any (
    "MSExchangeOWAAppPool","MSExchangeECPAppPool","MSExchangeServicesAppPool",
    "MSExchangeOABAppPool","MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool")
| where NewProcessName has_any (
    "\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\net.exe","\\net1.exe",
    "\\whoami.exe","\\ipconfig.exe","\\systeminfo.exe","\\nltest.exe",
    "\\quser.exe","\\tasklist.exe","\\procdump.exe","\\procdump64.exe",
    "\\rundll32.exe","\\7z.exe","\\7za.exe","\\certutil.exe",
    "\\bitsadmin.exe","\\curl.exe","\\wget.exe","\\makecab.exe")
| extend Severity = case(
    CommandLine has_any ("lsass","comsvcs.dll","MiniDump"), "Critical-LSASS",
    CommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","-enc ","DownloadString","IEX("), "High-PSCradle",
    NewProcessName has_any ("\\7z.exe","\\makecab.exe") and CommandLine has_any (".pst",".eml",".aspx","Mailbox"), "High-Archive",
    "Medium-Recon")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, Severity, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Exchange Webshell Post-Exploitation Child Process
id: 5b1e4f1d-7f8c-4d8a-b5a4-9a4f88e0c3a1
status: experimental
description: Detects IIS Exchange application pool worker (w3wp.exe) spawning recon, credential-dump, archive or LOLBin egress utilities — the canonical post-ProxyLogon webshell behaviour attributed to HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon (Operation Exchange Marauder).
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_apppool:
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MSExchangeOWAAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeECPAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeServicesAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeOABAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeRpcProxyAppPool'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\7za.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
      - '\makecab.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_apppool and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Exchange administrator scripts run from IIS context (rare; should be allowlisted by hash/cmdline).
  - Exchange health-check scripts launched by Microsoft monitoring agents.
level: critical
ucs.rationale: This is HAFNIUM's signature kill chain — Microsoft's MSTIC, CISA AA21-062A, FireEye/Mandiant, and Volexity all describe identical w3wp.exe → cmd/powershell/procdump tradecraft following ProxyLogon webshell drops. Pinning the parent to Exchange-specific app pools (MSExchangeOWAAppPool etc.) eliminates almost all benign IIS noise — legitimate Exchange code paths do not shell out to procdump/7z/certu

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon service-principal credential addition + first-time MS Graph access from new IP (cloud-pivot tradecraft)
ucs.description: Hunts Silk Typhoon's 2024–2025 pivot from on-prem Exchange to cloud: after harvesting admin tokens or abusing zero-days in IT supply-chain products (Ivanti CSA, Palo Alto PAN-OS, Citrix NetScaler, Commvault), the actor adds a new credential (secret/cert) to an existing OAuth application or service principal, then authenticates as that SP to MS Graph/Exchange Online from new infrastructure to enumerate mail and OneDrive. Microsoft's March 2025 Silk Typhoon advisory specifically calls out this T1098.001 → T1550.001 → T1078.004 chain.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as credAddTime from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where All_Changes.action=modified (All_Changes.object_category="Service Principal" OR All_Changes.object_category="Application") (All_Changes.command="Add service principal credentials" OR All_Changes.command="Update application*Certificates and secrets management*" OR All_Changes.command="Update service principal") by All_Changes.user All_Changes.object All_Changes.src All_Changes.command
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Changes)`
| rename object as AppName
| join type=inner AppName [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count as spSignIns dc(Authentication.src) as srcIPs values(Authentication.src) as signin_ips min(_time) as firstSpSignIn from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.signature_id=ServicePrincipalSignIn Authentication.action=success by Authentication.user Authentication.app Authentication.src
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)`
    | rename app as AppName user as ServicePrincipalId
  ]
| where firstSpSignIn >= credAddTime AND firstSpSignIn <= (credAddTime + 86400)
| eval delta_minutes=round((firstSpSignIn-credAddTime)/60,1)
| table credAddTime firstSpSignIn delta_minutes user AppName ServicePrincipalId src signin_ips command
| sort - credAddTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon — credential added to an OAuth app/SP, then that SP authenticates from a new IP within 24h
let Lookback = 14d;
let CorrelationWindow = 24h;
// 1) Service-principal credential additions seen in CloudAppEvents (O365 audit)
let CredAdds = CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("Add service principal credentials.",
                          "Update application \u2013 Certificates and secrets management.",
                          "Update service principal.",
                          "Add application.")
    | extend AppId = tostring(parse_json(tostring(RawEventData)).Target[0].ID)
    | extend AppName = tostring(ObjectName)
    | project CredAddTime = Timestamp, ActorUpn = AccountDisplayName,
              ActorIP = IPAddress, ActorCountry = CountryCode,
              AppName, AppId, ActionType, RawEventData;
// 2) Per-app baseline: IPs that have signed in as this SP in the prior 30d
let SpBaseline = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp between (ago(60d) .. ago(Lookback))
    | where isnotempty(ApplicationId)
    | summarize KnownIPs = make_set(IPAddress, 500), KnownCountries = make_set(Country, 50)
              by ApplicationId;
// 3) Recent SP sign-ins to Microsoft Graph after the credential was added
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where ResourceId == "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000"   // Microsoft Graph
| where ErrorCode == 0
| where TokenIssuerType == "AzureAD"
| where isnotempty(ApplicationId)
| join kind=inner CredAdds on $left.ApplicationId == $right.AppId
| where Timestamp between (CredAddTime .. CredAddTime + CorrelationWindow)
| join kind=leftouter SpBaseline on ApplicationId
| extend NewIP = iif(isnull(KnownIPs) or not(set_has_element(KnownIPs, IPAddress)), true, false),
         NewCountry = iif(isnull(KnownCountries) or not(set_has_element(KnownCountries, Country)), true, false)
| where NewIP == true or NewCountry == true
| project SignInTime = Timestamp, AppName, ApplicationId, IPAddress, Country, City,
          NewIP, NewCountry, CredAddTime, ActorUpn, ActorIP, ActorCountry, ActionType
| order by SignInTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon — AAD credential add on existing SP/App followed by SP sign-in from new IP
let Lookback = 14d;
let CorrelationWindow = 24h;
let CredAdds = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where OperationName in ("Add service principal credentials",
                              "Update application \u2013 Certificates and secrets management",
                              "Update service principal",
                              "Add application")
    | where Result =~ "success"
    | extend Initiator = coalesce(
        tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
        tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).app.displayName))
    | extend InitiatorIP = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress)
    | mv-expand TargetResources
    | extend AppName = tostring(TargetResources.displayName),
             AppId = tostring(TargetResources.id),
             KeyAdded = tostring(TargetResources.modifiedProperties)
    | where KeyAdded has_any ("KeyDescription","PasswordCredentials","KeyCredentials")
    | project CredAddTime = TimeGenerated, OperationName, Initiator, InitiatorIP, AppName, AppId;
let SpBaseline = union isfuzzy=true
    (AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs | where TimeGenerated between (ago(60d) .. ago(Lookback)) | project AppId, IPAddress),
    (AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs | where TimeGenerated between (ago(60d) .. ago(Lookback)) | project AppId, IPAddress)
    | summarize KnownIPs = make_set(IPAddress, 500) by AppId;
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where ResultType == 0
| where ResourceId == "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000"  // Microsoft Graph
| join kind=inner CredAdds on AppId
| where TimeGenerated between (CredAddTime .. CredAddTime + CorrelationWindow)
| join kind=leftouter SpBaseline on AppId
| extend NewIP = iif(isnull(KnownIPs) or not(set_has_element(KnownIPs, IPAddress)), true, false)
| where NewIP == true
| project SignInTime = TimeGenerated, ServicePrincipalName, AppId, IPAddress, Location,
          ResourceDisplayName, CredAddTime, OperationName, Initiator, InitiatorIP, AppName
| order by SignInTime desc
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's March 2025 Silk Typhoon advisory and CISA reporting specifically describe this exact two-stage cloud pivot: (1) the actor adds an additional credential to an already-trusted OAuth app or service principal it gained control of via stolen admin tokens or supply-chain zero-day (Ivanti CSA CVE-2024-7593, Palo Alto PAN-OS, Citrix NetScaler, Commvault), then (2) authenticates as the SP to MS
